transcript.text: I’ve finished the runtime audit: there are no app providers, CSS imports, network queries, browser-state reads, or portals to mirror. The real environment is the chart registration flow itself, so I’m moving into Storybook setup and then I’ll add chart stories one file at a time, running the Storybook Vitest project after each file before I move on.

I’ve got enough context to edit Storybook safely now. I’m replacing the scaffold preview with a library-specific default environment, narrowing discovery away from the onboarding docs, and removing the placeholder example stories so the new source stories are the ones Vitest exercises.

I’m making one small preview adjustment before the first story: the library is renderer-agnostic, so I’m choosing the DOM-visible SVG renderer in Storybook. That keeps the app code untouched and gives us much stronger `play` assertions than a canvas-only surface.
I caught a preview typing hiccup from that renderer switch. I’m cleaning that up first so the first story starts from a stable base instead of carrying a stray type error forward.

I’m starting with `LineChart` because it exercises the core library path: direct options, composed child components, and `compose` with another chart type. The goal for the first file is to validate both the renderer registration in preview and the context-based option composition.

The first story file is in place and Vitest is running against it now. I’m watching for the real browser-mode failure mode rather than guessing; if anything breaks, I’ll fix preview or story assertions before I add the next file.

I’m pivoting those stories back to the stable public option props and keeping `compose` coverage where the library actually behaves.

I’m moving that registration into preview now so the stories can stay clean and the default environment does the setup once.

content.text:
Storybook now uses a package-faithful shared runtime in [.storybook/preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-40-49Z-72d6999f/project/.storybook/preview.tsx) and a cleaner story discovery setup in [.storybook/main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-40-49Z-72d6999f/project/.storybook/main.ts). After tracing the codebase through the exports and internals, there turned out to be no app providers, root CSS, browser-state reads, fetches, or portals to reproduce, so I kept the default environment small: global ECharts registration for the renderer and common tree-shaken components. I also removed the placeholder scaffold stories so Storybook focuses on real library surfaces.

Added seven colocated story files for the primary chart wrappers, each with three stories and a `play` function: [src/LineChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-40-49Z-72d6999f/project/src/LineChart.stories.tsx), [src/BarChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-40-49Z-72d6999f/project/src/BarChart.stories.tsx), [src/PieChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-40-49Z-72d6999f/project/src/PieChart.stories.tsx), [src/ScatterChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-40-49Z-72d6999f/project/src/ScatterChart.stories.tsx), [src/EffectScatterChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-40-49Z-72d6999f/project/src/EffectScatterChart.stories.tsx), [src/GraphChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-40-49Z-72d6999f/project/src/GraphChart.stories.tsx), and [src/HeatmapChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T15-40-49Z-72d6999f/project/src/HeatmapChart.stories.tsx). I used real public-library patterns like direct ECharts option props, `compose`, `dataset`, and `use={GridComponent}` while keeping all runtime setup in preview.

Verification is green: `npx vitest --project storybook src/*.stories.tsx` passed with 21/21 tests, and `npx tsc --noEmit` passed. No `preview-body.html` or MSW setup was needed because this codebase doesn’t use portals or network queries.
